content: 2:14am Feb 11 — auth migration rollout failed. Fallback path didn't preserve org_id in session token. 11 minutes to full rollback. Ari declared SEV-1, comms to Acme and Initech within 8min.
summary: Auth rollout failed; org_id lost in fallback path

content: When auth incident occurs: (1) Ari or Devon declares SEV level immediately, (2) Marcus notifies top 3 customers within 5min, (3) Sebastien gets pinged via 1Password alert, (4) RCA owner assigned before rollback completes.
Ari pinged Slack 2:47am with rollback confirmation. No data loss, session cache refreshed within 3min. Globex reported they didn't notice the gap. Wayne Industries asked for a status page update.
summary: Rollback confirmed; minimal customer impact observed

content: Fallback auth path must preserve org_id as immutable claim in JWT. Previous design passed it as mutable session attribute only. This is non-negotiable for multi-tenant consistency.
summary: JWT org_id must be immutable claim, not session attr

content: RCA template for auth incidents: include (1) timeline of rollback events, (2) root cause with code refs, (3) what monitoring missed, (4) remediation steps before re-deploy, (5) sign-off from Ari + Devon.
summary: RCA template for auth incidents finalized

content: Ari finished RCA draft Feb 12 evening. Root cause: migration script tested in staging with only 1 org. Fallback logic never hit in prod load-test. He noted Tom didn't catch it in code review.
summary: RCA draft done; staging didn't catch single-org edge case

content: Staging must mirror prod org structure (min 3 test orgs with cross-org requests). Single-org test suites mask fallback path bugs. This applies to any customer-isolation logic.

Before any auth rollout: (1) Ari reviews fallback path explicitly, (2) load-test includes org context switching, (3) DataDog alerting on org_id null values in audit log, (4) Sentry dashboard for session validation errors.
I spend too much time re-explaining why staging coverage matters. This Feb 11 incident proves it cleanly. Need to write a doc so I stop repeating this.
summary: Pattern: re-explaining staging scope. Will document once.

content: Postmortem published Feb 14 10am. Sebastien asked about customer comms timeline — Marcus had already sent detailed explanations to Acme, Initech, Globex. Wayne Industries asked for SLA credit; Anya approved $2400.
summary: Postmortem published; $2400 credit issued to Wayne

content: For 11-minute outages affecting customer-facing APIs, SLA credits are standard if incident was internal ops error (not vendor/external). $200/min baseline for enterprise; Anya has discretion to adjust.
summary: SLA credit policy: $200/min baseline for ops errors

content: Audit log queries for auth incidents: (1) SELECT * FROM session_tokens WHERE org_id IS NULL, (2) check Cloudflare cache hit rate during rollback window, (3) cross-ref Anthropic token usage logs for stuck requests.
summary: Audit log queries for auth incident forensics

content: Ari mentioned Feb 15 that James asked good questions in RCA review about why fallback path wasn't feature-gated. Decided all future auth changes require explicit feature flag + gradual rollout (5%, 25%, 100%).
summary: James raised feature-flag question; gradual rollout now mandatory

content: RFC for auth changes must include fallback scenario testing and rollback timing. If fallback code can't be tested in staging, the rollout plan itself is suspect.
summary: Auth RFC requires fallback scenario testing

content: Post-incident: (1) update Linear with blocking issues before re-deploy, (2) Tom to attend code review sessions on auth until 3 PRs merged, (3) Devon pair with Ari on next auth rollout, (4) Anya audit Sentry budget for increased logging.
summary: Follow-up tasks: code review, pairing, monitoring budget

content: The Feb 11 incident felt avoidable but not surprising—we've been scaling fast and cutting corners on staging parity. I'm less frustrated now that we have a concrete RFC template and Tom has clear growth path.
summary: Incident felt avoidable; concrete fixes reduce my stress
